title: Crispy Wholemeal Polish Wholemeal Flour Pancakes
description: Pungent, crispy, cozy polish dish featuring strong wholemeal flour, great for breakfast or brunch. It's ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly and can be made in a wok.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine wholemeal flour, buckwheat flour, sugar, and yeast.
Mix in lukewarm water to form a smooth batter.
Let the batter rest for 1 hour and allow it to rise.
Heat a non-stick wok or frying pan over medium-high heat.
Brush the wok with sesame oil to prevent sticking.
Drop 1/4 cup of the batter into the wok, spreading it evenly to form a pancake.
Cook for 2-3 minutes on the first side, until bubbles start to appear and the edges start to dry.
Flip the pancake and cook for another 2 minutes, until golden brown.
Repeat with the remaining batter, until all pancakes are cooked.
Brush the pancakes with melted ghee or butter, and sprinkle with coarse salt.
Garnish with chopped fresh dill and serve warm.
